name: Alexander Shenkin
school: School of Informatics, Computing, Cyber Systems
focus: Forest ecology • Canopy structure • Carbon cycling
bio: Alexander F. Shenkin is an Assistant Research Professor at Northern Arizona University whose work focuses on forest ecology, ecosystem structure, and global carbon cycling. His research investigates how tree and canopy traits scale across ecosystems and influence environmental processes, particularly in tropical forests. Dr. Shenkin integrates remote sensing technologies, field observations, and ecological modeling to better understand plant function, forest dynamics, and responses to climate change and drought. His work contributes to improving predictions of ecosystem behavior under changing environmental conditions. With a background that bridges engineering and ecological science, he is also involved in developing innovative environmental sensing and autonomous systems for studying complex natural environments.
